key_points: Chamisa dorms have recurring hot water outages lasting over a week
On-campus housing described as expensive for what it offers, often leaky
The Verge off-campus apartments: party central, safety hazards, avoid it
key_quotes: I currently live in Chamisa G building, and haven't had hot water for more than a week
It's expensive for what it is (usually leaky units with a multitude of secondary issues)
I currently live here at the Verge with my girlfriend. Avoid it. Holy shit please fucking avoid the Verge. This place is honestly a hell hole and a safety hazard

key_points: Making friends is genuinely hard; many students feel isolated on weekends
Campus energy and school pride are weaker than comparable D1 schools
Online classes have hollowed out in-person campus community significantly
key_quotes: I just kinda… don't make friends kinda hard here
Online classes have sucked a lot of the oxygen out of the campus
NMSU won't have pride like UMich or UCONN because while we're a D1 school, we lack the funding

key_points: Frenger dining hall has limited app integration, hard to order remotely
Kiwibot delivery exists but ordering system is broken for some buildings
No standout praise or condemnation; dining largely goes unmentioned
key_quotes: Does anyone know why Frenger doesn't show up on the everyday app? There's a kiwi stop in front but I can't find any way to order something from there
Let's see the inside of the Cafeteria more

key_points: Free therapy available through NMSU Family Resource Center, no insurance needed
Sleep deprivation is a real issue; students petitioned for sleep pods
Support resources exist but students seem unaware of them
key_quotes: A lot of students at New Mexico State struggle with sleep deprivation—whether it's from POTS, commuting, working through school, or just the chaos of college life
The NMSU Family Resource Center offers free therapy services to individuals, couples, and families in the community. No insurance required

key_points: Disbursement delays are a recurring, multi-semester problem causing real hardship
New Mexico Opportunity Scholarship covers full tuition for NM residents at 2.5 GPA
Financial aid system is confusing; balances fluctuate unexpectedly post-disbursement
key_quotes: Was gonna buy a laptop but got screwed over by delays
I went from a refund to now owing $197.71 wtf. Their system is hella messed up
New Mexico has 100% free tuition for all residents at public institutions

key_points: Engineering and aerospace are the standout programs; ABET-accredited and employer-respected
CMI (creative media) coursework is extremely heavy; four classes is described as brutal
Online M.Eng. and other grad programs are quality-variable but improving
key_quotes: Engineering is the same everywhere. I have several friends who graduated from NMSU and went to work for Northrop Grumman, General Dynamics
The workload has already been kind of crazy. Should I drop a class for the sake of my sanity?
I have peers that went to other schools and I think they are ahead a bit skill wise but generally it's a decent program

key_points: Housing and financial aid offices are slow to respond and hard to reach by phone
Parking enforcement is aggressive and reportedly inaccurate; appeals process exists but is adversarial
Administration uses AI-generated content despite having a full art department
key_quotes: If this is your first experience dealing with what this university has in terms of a 'website', this is going to be a long journey
Sorry to hear that NMSU still has parking Nazis. They desperately need to build parking garages
Not NMSU using AI when they have a whole art department

key_points: Las Cruces is affordable, low-density, and close to El Paso and the outdoors
Campus is visually appealing but parking is a persistent, semester-long headache
El Paso is 40 minutes away for city amenities; Juárez is similarly close
key_quotes: Las Cruces is a pretty sweet place, not too many people and close to El Paso
Parking has been absolutely horrible this semester. I purchased a permit, but I'm paying at meters more than I am finding reasonable parking
If you enjoy the outdoors then there is a lot to do

key_points: Engineering grads regularly land at Northrop Grumman, General Dynamics, and defense firms
Most engineering grads stay local to NM/TX region; national reach is limited
Journalism program has solid regional placement in El Paso and Albuquerque markets
key_quotes: NMSU engineering graduates get jobs in aeronautic, defense, high tech, and oil industry companies and these entities actively hire from NMSU via career fairs
Most engineering grads stay local to the area they went to school in
Their Journalism school has pumped out a lot of TV reporters and producers over the years

key_points: NM residents pay essentially zero tuition via Opportunity Scholarship at 2.5 GPA
Out-of-state students pay significantly more with less clear ROI than in-state peers
One of the cheapest M.Eng. and M.S. programs nationally; strong for working professionals
key_quotes: NMSU is cheaper, which is wild to me [compared to Oregon State out-of-state]
In all honesty, I feel like people coming out of state to attend a college that they could probably attend in-state with the same degree, isn't the best financial choice
This may be the cheapest program in the country and I am for that

key_points: Friendly, family-oriented culture but lacks energy of larger flagship universities
Strong sense of nostalgia and alumni loyalty despite lower national profile
Campus feel has improved over 30 years but still trails peer D1 schools in buzz
key_quotes: I love NMSU and the school is truly family oriented
I feel like the sense of school pride and school community has grown in those 30 years
After also earning my master's from another school, I've come to realize something was missing at NMSU

key_points: **Safety**: LGBTQ employee made gun threats at event; administration closed case quietly
**Housing**: Chamisa dorms lack hot water for weeks; facilities unresponsive to complaints
**Administration**: Phishing scams using NMSU accounts reported; IT lockouts followed reports
key_quotes: If you're queer or an ally don't go to events at NMSU; employee allowed to make gun violence threats. The matter has been closed. Oh well
I reported it and for my service I have been locked out of my account until tomorrow when IT is back
OIE is an internal institution check that will almost always side with the institution

key_points: DACC dental clinic offers full cleanings, X-rays, and exams for just $40
Activity center recently renovated with new equipment; free for full-time students
Wine tasting course counts as a VWW credit requirement — genuinely popular
key_quotes: Students pay a $40 flat fee for dental cleaning, exam, x-rays, and more
Recent renovations also, so the workout area and equipment is new
Wine tasting if it's still available. Took that as a VWW, was able to drink with a good group of people with decent food every Wednesday
